title: 'podman run {imageid}' causes errors in 'podman ps'
body:
kind bug

**Description**
The command

'podman run {imageid}' errors out as it should, but then the command 'podman ps --all' shows a number of artifact errors.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  podman pull alpine

2. podman run {alpineImageId}

3.  podman ps --all

**Describe the results you received:**
When doing 'podman ps --all' afterwards, you see:

```
# podman ps --all
ERRO[0000] couldn't get some ps information, error getting artifact "0043d1a4792d0bf3f6bb2e7370239b6b594a59ecad56f897b5f25ba355c46ab7": open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/0043d1a4792d0bf3f6bb2e7370239b6b594a59ecad56f897b5f25ba355c46ab7/userdata/artifacts/create-config: no such file or directory
ERRO[0000] couldn't get some ps information, error getting artifact "3d1b99a77ff55f0cc8540a716a13ade6d0be04a4ec3693ed2be3f9e61899a15a": open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/3d1b99a77ff55f0cc8540a716a13ade6d0be04a4ec3693ed2be3f9e61899a15a/userdata/artifacts/create-config: no such file or directory
ERRO[0000] couldn't get some ps information, error getting artifact "6d86e881ede775def709a364ac2462c0df22376b54ba9e5715c0075e7f3cd80f": open /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay-containers/6d86e881ede775def709a364ac2462c0df22376b54ba9e5715c0075e7f3cd80f/userdata/artifacts/create-config: no such file or directory
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE          COMMAND   CREATED AT                      STATUS                          PORTS   NAMES
0043d1a4792d   422dc563ca32             2018-02-14 12:41:58 -0500 EST   Created                                 brave_roentgen
3d1b99a77ff5   422dc563ca32             2018-02-14 12:41:36 -0500 EST   Created                                 cranky_cray
6d86e881ede7   422dc563ca32             2018-02-14 12:43:27 -0500 EST   Created                                 hardcore_engelbart
db7501115f90   422dc563ca32   /bin/sh   2018-02-14 12:42:14 -0500 EST   Exited (0) About a minute ago           suspicious_nobel
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

No errors in the 'podman ps --all' command and the containers that "failed to create" should not be listed.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Very repeatable

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# podman version
Version:       
Go Version:    go1.9.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

VM running the latest Fedora 27 and using the Podman kit undergoing testing.

comments.body: Per discussion with @baude it sounds like we should be bailing in container creation, but it is allowed to succeed and create the container, even though it cannot be run.
FYI, pastebin with the errors:  http://pastebin.test.redhat.com/555856
@TomSweeneyRedHat this should be fixed now.  care to verify and close?

You made it work on Ubuntu even!
```
# podman run fedora
No command specified on command line or as CMD or ENTRYPOINT in this image
# podman ps 
# podman ps --all
``

Closing and thanks @baude!
